body: Hey, I noticed a charge from OPI Professional Supply hit the business account this morning — $342. Did you approve this order, or was it supposed to be broken into two separate payments? Just want to make sure I'm categorizing it correctly before I close out this week's records. Let me know when you get a chance!
snippet: Hey, I noticed a charge from OPI Professional Supply hit the business account th...
subject: Supplier invoice

body: Yes, that's totally legit! I placed an order last Tuesday for a restock of gel base coats and a few seasonal colors. It was supposed to be around $312 but I added some extra bottles at checkout. I'll forward you the order confirmation so you can attach it to the expense entry. Go ahead and categorize it under supplies — I'll add a more specific note when I sit down with the books this weekend.
snippet: Yes, that's totally legit! I placed an order last Tuesday for a restock of gel b...
subject: Re: Supplier invoice

body: Hey, Jasmine texted me asking about next week's schedule — I think she still has my number saved from when you were out sick last month. She wants to know if she's on for Saturday afternoon because she has a family event in the morning. Can you check your schedule and reach out to her? Also, are you still short-handed on Thursdays?
snippet: Hey, Jasmine texted me asking about next week's schedule — I think she still has...
subject: Staffing schedule

body: Ugh, I keep forgetting to update the contact sheet! Yes, she's on for Saturday at 1pm so she should be fine as long as her morning wraps up by noon. Thursdays are still a mess — I'm covering the 10–4 slot myself but the late shift is wide open. I posted on the local Facebook group last week but haven't gotten any serious responses yet.
snippet: Ugh, I keep forgetting to update the contact sheet! Yes, she's on for Saturday a...
subject: Re: Staffing schedule

body: Got it, I'll let Jasmine know about Saturday. On the Thursday situation — what about that girl who came in for a trial shift last month? Mia or Mya, I think? She seemed really enthusiastic. It might be worth reaching out to her directly rather than waiting on the Facebook post. Even a part-time person for Thursdays and Fridays would make a big difference heading into the busy season.
snippet: Got it, I'll let Jasmine know about Saturday. On the Thursday situation — what a...
subject: Re: Staffing schedule

body: Hey, just a heads-up — Q2 estimated taxes are due June 15th. I know you've been slammed at the salon but we need to set aside the payment this week. Based on last quarter I'm estimating we owe around $1,800 for the business side. Do you want me to pull the income figures from the past few months so we can verify the number before submitting?
snippet: Hey, just a heads-up — Q2 estimated taxes are due June 15th. I know you've been ...
subject: Quarterly tax estimate

body: Yes please, that would be really helpful. April and May felt busier than Q1 so the number might be a bit higher. All my appointment totals are in the Square dashboard — if you log in you can export a report. Just set the date range to April 1 through May 31 and it should pull everything cleanly.
snippet: Yes please, that would be really helpful. April and May felt busier than Q1 so t...
subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate

body: Pulled the numbers — gross revenue for April and May came to $14,340. After expenses, taxable income for the quarter looks like roughly $8,200, which puts our estimated payment closer to $2,050. I'll fill out the 1040-ES today and we can pay through IRS Direct Pay. Do you want to review it first or should I just go ahead and submit?
snippet: Pulled the numbers — gross revenue for April and May came to $14,340. After expe...
subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate

body: Go ahead and submit — I trust your math completely! Just save the confirmation number somewhere safe. I'll log the payment in the business ledger when I get home tonight. One favor: can you remind me about next quarter a couple of weeks earlier? I hate cutting it this close every time. Thanks for handling all this, I really appreciate it.
snippet: Go ahead and submit — I trust your math completely! Just save the confirmation n...
subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate

body: Hey, did you see the new Google review that came in this morning? It's two stars from someone listed as L. Patterson — she says her gel manicure started chipping after four days and that nobody at the salon acknowledged her concern when she came back in. It's pretty visible on your profile right now. Want me to draft a response so you can review it?
snippet: Hey, did you see the new Google review that came in this morning? It's two stars...
subject: Customer review reply

body: I saw it — I was honestly really upset. I think I know who this is. She came in on a Tuesday and left without saying anything to me, then returned Thursday and spoke to the front desk. I'm not sure it got flagged properly. I do want to respond but I don't want to come across as defensive. Please draft something and I'll edit it before we post.
snippet: I saw it — I was honestly really upset. I think I know who this is. She came in ...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body: Here's a draft: 'Thank you for your feedback, L. We're sorry to hear your gel manicure didn't last as long as expected — that's not the experience we want for any of our guests. We'd love the chance to make this right. Please reach out to us at cecilia.burnett@goldenprairienails.com and we'll schedule a complimentary touch-up at your convenience.' Short, professional — what do you think?
snippet: Here's a draft: 'Thank you for your feedback, L. We're sorry to hear your gel ma...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body: I really like it! I'd add one line about our quality guarantee so other readers see it — something like 'We stand behind the quality of every service we provide.' Just drop it before the last sentence. Otherwise it's perfect. Go ahead and post the updated version whenever you're ready. Hopefully she reaches out so we can fix things properly.
snippet: I really like it! I'd add one line about our quality guarantee so other readers ...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body: Updated and posted! The final version reads: 'Thank you for your feedback, L. We're sorry your gel manicure didn't hold up as expected — that's not the experience we want for any of our guests. We stand behind the quality of every service we provide and we'd love the chance to make this right. Please reach out to us at cecilia.burnett@goldenprairienails.com to schedule a complimentary touch-up at your convenience.' Let me know if she contacts you directly.
snippet: Updated and posted! The final version reads: 'Thank you for your feedback, L. We...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body: Cecilia! It feels like forever since we've actually sat down together without a million distractions. Are you free for lunch any day this week? I've been dying to try that new Mediterranean place on Elm Street — I drove past it last weekend and it looked adorable. Any day works for me except Thursday, so just say the word!
snippet: Cecilia! It feels like forever since we've actually sat down together without a ...
subject: Lunch this week?

body: Sharon, yes — absolutely yes. I desperately need a real lunch break that doesn't involve eating a protein bar over my appointment book. Wednesday works perfectly, I have a gap between clients from noon to 2. Let's do the Mediterranean spot, I've been wanting to try it too. Should we say 12:15 so we can grab a table before the rush? I can't wait!
snippet: Sharon, yes — absolutely yes. I desperately need a real lunch break that doesn't...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body: Hey! I know we literally just had lunch but something's been on my mind and I need a real conversation, not a text thread. Are you free for coffee next Tuesday morning? Maybe around 9 or 9:30? That little bakery on Maple has the most incredible lattes and it's never too crowded that early. Say yes!
snippet: Hey! I know we literally just had lunch but something's been on my mind and I ne...
subject: Coffee next Tuesday?

body: Of course — now you have me very curious! Tuesday at 9:30 works perfectly, I don't have my first client until 11 so we'd have a full hour at least. Maple Street Bakery is perfect. Should I be worried, or is this a good thing? I'm already mentally preparing both reactions. Either way, I'm there.
snippet: Of course — now you have me very curious! Tuesday at 9:30 works perfectly, I don...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body: Ha! Don't panic, it's a good thing — at least I think so. I just really want your honest opinion before I commit to anything. You know I trust your judgment more than anyone else's. See you Tuesday! I'll already be on my second coffee when you arrive, fair warning. Bring your positive energy just in case I need a pep talk.
snippet: Ha! Don't panic, it's a good thing — at least I think so. I just really want you...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body: Okay, Marcus's birthday is the 14th and I am NOT letting it go low-key this year like I did last year. I want to do a proper brunch — somewhere nice but not stuffy. I was thinking Rosewood Bistro because bottomless mimosas and Marcus secretly loves brunch even though he pretends to be too cool for it. Are you and Craig free that Sunday?
snippet: Okay, Marcus's birthday is the 14th and I am NOT letting it go low-key this year...
subject: Birthday brunch plans

body: Craig and I would absolutely love to come! Rosewood is such a great choice — that patio is gorgeous in the summer. I'm blocking it off on our calendar right now before Craig fills the weekend with something. Should I RSVP for four or are you inviting more people? Also, do you want to go in together on a gift? I already have a few ideas brewing.
snippet: Craig and I would absolutely love to come! Rosewood is such a great choice — tha...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body: Just the four of us — Marcus doesn't do well with big groups and I want it to feel relaxed and fun. A joint gift is a great idea! I was thinking either a nice whiskey set or tickets to that jazz festival next month. He mentioned it casually a few weeks ago which means he really wants to go. What were your ideas? Let's pick the best one together.
snippet: Just the four of us — Marcus doesn't do well with big groups and I want it to fe...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body: I was actually thinking whiskey too, but honestly the jazz festival tickets are a better call since it's an experience you can't replicate. Let's do that! I'll look up ticket options tonight and send you the link. If we each put in $50 we should be able to get a great pair with a little left for a nice card. I'm already so excited for this brunch — it's going to be such a fun morning.
snippet: I was actually thinking whiskey too, but honestly the jazz festival tickets are ...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body: Hey, got a notice that our Vagaro subscription renews at the end of the month. They're offering a new annual plan at $85/month versus the $95 we pay now — about $120 in annual savings. I also came across a competitor called GlossGenius that a few nail salon owners seem to recommend. Do you want to just renew Vagaro or take a look at switching? No rush yet but wanted to flag it early.
snippet: Hey, got a notice that our Vagaro subscription renews at the end of the month. T...
subject: Renewal options

body: I've heard GlossGenius come up a few times — I think Tanya from the salon on Fifth uses it and loves it. Can you look into their plans and compare features? The $120 savings on Vagaro is nice but if GlossGenius has better automated reminders or a cleaner checkout flow it could be worth the switch even at a slightly higher price. I've had a few issues with Vagaro's client messaging lately.
snippet: I've heard GlossGenius come up a few times — I think Tanya from the salon on Fif...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: Looked it up — GlossGenius starts at $48/month for basic and $83/month for the full suite. Reviews consistently mention a better mobile experience and stronger automated appointment reminders, which I know has been a frustration with Vagaro. Switching would save more money annually. The main downside is migrating your client list, but I can help with that over a weekend if you decide to go for it.
snippet: Looked it up — GlossGenius starts at $48/month for basic and $83/month for the f...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: The client migration sounds annoying but doable. Better appointment reminders alone might be worth it — I've had a handful of no-shows lately that a well-timed text could have prevented. Can you reach out to GlossGenius and ask if they have a free trial? I'd really want to test the interface before fully committing to a switch. If the trial feels good, let's go for it.
snippet: The client migration sounds annoying but doable. Better appointment reminders al...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: Good news — they offer a 14-day free trial with no credit card required. I'll set it up tonight using the business email. That gives us until mid-month to test everything before the Vagaro renewal deadline hits. I'll also check whether Vagaro charges any cancellation fee so there are no surprises. Everything should be ready for you to explore by tomorrow morning.
snippet: Good news — they offer a 14-day free trial with no credit card required. I'll se...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: Hey, I'm going through the bank statement and there's a charge for $178 from 'CND Professional' that I can't match to any invoice in our records folder. Do you remember ordering from them recently? It might be from that phone order you placed a few weeks ago. Just want to verify it before I close out the month — don't want an unmatched charge sitting there.
snippet: Hey, I'm going through the bank statement and there's a charge for $178 from 'CN...
subject: Vendor invoice question

body: Yes, that's from an order for shellac top coats and some nail prep items I called in a while back. The invoice came through by email — check the business inbox, it might have landed in the promotions folder. The total sounds about right, maybe a dollar or two off because they gave me a small discount. I'll dig through my emails on my end too and forward it to you if I find it first.
snippet: Yes, that's from an order for shellac top coats and some nail prep items I calle...
subject: Re: Vendor invoice question

body: Our lease is up at the end of August and the management office sent over the renewal terms this morning. Options are: 12-month renewal at $2,150/month (up from $2,050), month-to-month at $2,300, or a 24-month lock-in at $2,080. Given that we've been loosely talking about buying in the next year or two, I'm not sure which makes the most sense. Wanted to get your thoughts before responding to them.
snippet: Our lease is up at the end of August and the management office sent over the ren...
subject: Apartment lease renewal

body: That's a tough call. I really don't want to be locked into 24 months if buying is actually on the table — but month-to-month at $2,300 feels like a lot of extra money for flexibility we might not even use. Can we sit down tonight and go over it? I want to look at where we actually are with the down payment savings and figure out how realistic buying in the next year is before we commit to anything. Let's not sign yet.
snippet: That's a tough call. I really don't want to be locked into 24 months if buying i...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body: Agreed, let's talk tonight before deciding anything. I'll pull together our current savings picture and look at what a pre-approval process might look like for us. Worth noting — the letter says they need a response by July 15th, so we have about two weeks. I'll also call the management office tomorrow to ask if a 6-month option is available; sometimes they'll negotiate on that. See you at home.
snippet: Agreed, let's talk tonight before deciding anything. I'll pull together our curr...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body: Hey! I know you barely have time to open your own inbox so I'm flagging two articles I thought you'd genuinely want to read. First is a piece on managing seasonal cash flow for small businesses — really relevant given how summer tends to slow down for salons. Second is a nail industry trends report for Q3 2026. Dropping both links below. You can thank me later.
snippet: Hey! I know you barely have time to open your own inbox so I'm flagging two arti...
subject: Newsletter — this week's reads

body: You are genuinely the best for doing this — I never would have seen either of these on my own. I read the cash flow piece on my lunch break and it had some really solid advice about building a 3-month operating reserve. We should actually look at whether our current cushion is there yet. The trends report is going on my Saturday morning reading list for sure.
snippet: You are genuinely the best for doing this — I never would have seen either of th...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body: Glad it was useful! On the cash flow point — I did some rough math and I think we're about one month short of that 3-month buffer. Not a crisis, but worth being intentional about over the next few months. Also, do you want me to just add you to this newsletter directly? You keep reading my forwards anyway and you'd get them a day earlier than I do.
snippet: Glad it was useful! On the cash flow point — I did some rough math and I think w...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body: Ha, yes — go ahead and subscribe me, I'll actually try to keep up with it! On the reserve gap, what if we automatically transfer an extra $300 into the business savings account each month? If we put it on autopilot starting in July we should be in a comfortable spot by September when bookings pick back up. Good thinking on all of this. Really.
snippet: Ha, yes — go ahead and subscribe me, I'll actually try to keep up with it! On th...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body: Hey, trying to nail down the pickup plan for this week. I have work calls on Tuesday and Thursday until 4:30 that I really can't move. School lets out at 3:45 on those days. Can you cover pickup? If Thursday doesn't work I can ask my mom but wanted to check with you first since I know your schedule shifted recently.
snippet: Hey, trying to nail down the pickup plan for this week. I have work calls on Tue...
subject: Schedule check — kids pickup

body: Tuesday works — I'll wrap up by 3:30 and head straight to school. If my last appointment runs a little long I'll bring them back to the salon, they're usually fine for 20–30 minutes with snacks and the iPad. Thursday is harder though, I have back-to-back clients until at least 5. Can your mom do Thursday? If not let me know and I'll see if I can shift something.
snippet: Tuesday works — I'll wrap up by 3:30 and head straight to school. If my last app...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body: I'll text my mom about Thursday — she loves any excuse to see them so I'm pretty confident she'll say yes. I'll confirm and let you know. Also, what about Friday? I was planning to handle it but just got an email about an optional team meeting at 4pm. I probably don't need to go, but wanted to flag it. I can absolutely skip it and do pickup if you need me to.
snippet: I'll text my mom about Thursday — she loves any excuse to see them so I'm pretty...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body: Go to the meeting if it's useful! I can handle Friday pickup too. It actually works out perfectly — Lily has been begging me to take her to the park after school on Fridays so we'll make a little afternoon of it. Just remind Noah about Saturday soccer practice tonight, please. I've told him twice already and I can tell it's not sticking.
snippet: Go to the meeting if it's useful! I can handle Friday pickup too. It actually wo...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body: Mom confirmed Thursday — she's even planning to take them for ice cream after so they'll be happy and fed when I pick them up around 6:30. And yes, I'll sit down with Noah tonight and make him write Saturday soccer in his planner myself. Thank you for covering Tuesday and Friday. You really do hold this whole operation together — I hope you know that.
snippet: Mom confirmed Thursday — she's even planning to take them for ice cream after so...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body: Okay, I finally carved out time to look at summer trip options and found some good deals for the last week of July. There's a beach house rental in Gulf Shores that sleeps eight — fits us plus my parents comfortably — for $1,900 for the full week. There's also a cabin option in the Smokies at $1,400, also with a pool. Both are available that week. Which direction are you leaning?
snippet: Okay, I finally carved out time to look at summer trip options and found some go...
subject: Family vacation planning

body: Beach, no contest. The kids have been asking about the ocean all spring and I think they're finally at the age where they'd really soak it in. Gulf Shores sounds perfect, and splitting the cost with your parents makes it totally reasonable. Can you check if the week of July 21 is still available for that house? I need to block those days off on the salon schedule as soon as possible — summer weeks fill up fast. Let's lock it in!
snippet: Beach, no contest. The kids have been asking about the ocean all spring and I th...
subject: Re: Family vacation planning

body: Hey, what's the plan this weekend? I don't think we have anything official on the calendar but I wanted to check before I told my buddy Derek we'd come to his backyard thing Saturday evening. It's super casual — burgers, lawn games, kids welcome. Nothing fancy at all. Let me know if that works or if you had something else in mind.
snippet: Hey, what's the plan this weekend? I don't think we have anything official on th...
subject: Weekend plans

body: Saturday evening works great! I'll be done at the salon by 4 so there's plenty of time to get home and get the kids sorted. Lawn games sound so fun — we haven't done anything like that in ages. What should I bring? I'm thinking a pasta salad or something sweet for dessert. Ask Derek if there's anything they actually need and I'll make that instead.
snippet: Saturday evening works great! I'll be done at the salon by 4 so there's plenty o...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body: Derek says bring whatever, they have plenty — but your pasta salad would be a guaranteed crowd-pleaser so definitely go with that. He also mentioned they might set up a slip and slide for the kids so pack a change of clothes and a couple of towels. I'm really looking forward to a low-key weekend. It feels like we've been running nonstop for weeks. See you Saturday!
snippet: Derek says bring whatever, they have plenty — but your pasta salad would be a gu...
subject: Re: Weekend plans
